characters.name: Peter Rabbit
characters.description: A small young rabbit wearing a blue jacket with brass buttons. Expressive, curious, impulsive. Often shown upright on hind legs.
characters.source: Peter Rabbit from Beatrix Potter's The Tale of Peter Rabbit (1902)

characters.name: Mrs. Rabbit
characters.description: Mother rabbit, larger than Peter. Wears an apron and sometimes a red cape. Warm, responsible, caring.
characters.source: Mrs. Rabbit (Old Mrs. Rabbit) from Beatrix Potter's The Tale of Peter Rabbit

characters.name: Flopsy, Mopsy, and Cotton-tail
characters.description: Peter's three sisters. Good, obedient young rabbits. Often shown together as a group.
characters.source: Flopsy, Mopsy, and Cotton-tail from Beatrix Potter's Peter Rabbit

characters.name: Mr. McGregor
characters.description: An old human gardener with a beard and glasses. Wears work clothes and boots. Carries a rake or hoe. The antagonist — he chases Peter.
characters.source: Mr. McGregor from Beatrix Potter's The Tale of Peter Rabbit

locations.name: The Rabbit Burrow
locations.description: A cozy home under a large fir tree. Interior has a fireplace, kitchen, table, beds. Warm and safe.

locations.name: The Woods
locations.description: Sandy path through woods near the burrow. Where Mrs. Rabbit walks to the baker's.

locations.name: Mr. McGregor's Garden
locations.description: A large vegetable garden with rows of lettuce, beans, radishes, carrots. Has flowerpots, a tool shed, a watering can, a gooseberry net, and a wooden gate. Surrounded by a wall.

pages.english_text: Peter lived with his family under a big tree.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_003.jpg] Rabbit family gathered outside their burrow under a large fir tree. Mother rabbit and four baby bunnies visible in a woodland setting. Warm, inviting opening scene.

pages.english_text: Mother dressed the little rabbits. Peter wore his blue jacket.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_004.jpg] Mrs. Rabbit in her apron dressing and fussing over the young rabbits indoors. Peter stands slightly apart, wearing his distinctive blue jacket with brass buttons.

pages.english_text: "Do not go into Mr. McGregor's garden!" said Mother.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_005.jpg] Mrs. Rabbit bending down in the woods, speaking seriously to Peter and his siblings. She is warning them, finger raised. The young rabbits look up at her.

pages.english_text: Then Mother went out with her basket.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_006.jpg] Mrs. Rabbit walking away through the woods carrying a basket and umbrella, wearing her red cape. She is heading off, leaving the children behind.

pages.english_text: The good rabbits picked blackberries in the lane.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_007.jpg] Three well-behaved young rabbits picking blackberries together from bushes. They are happy, obedient, doing exactly what Mother asked.

pages.english_text: But Peter ran straight to the garden!
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_008.jpg] Peter in his blue jacket squeezing his body under a wooden garden gate, sneaking into Mr. McGregor's garden. He looks determined and naughty — the moment of disobedience.

pages.english_text: Peter ate lettuce and beans and radishes.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_009.jpg] Peter standing upright among rows of vegetables, happily munching. A robin perches on a spade handle nearby, watching him. Peter looks content and greedy.

pages.english_text: He ate too much. His stomach hurt.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_010.jpg] Peter looking uncomfortable and sick, standing hunched by flowerpots. His expression shows he has overeaten — queasy and worried. First consequence of his greed.

pages.english_text: Mr. McGregor saw him! "Stop, thief!"
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_011.jpg] Mr. McGregor — old man with beard and glasses — bending down and spotting Peter among the vegetables. His expression is angry. Peter is caught!

pages.english_text: Peter was very scared. He ran and ran!
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_012.jpg] Peter sprinting away in terror. Mr. McGregor chasing behind him with a rake. Dynamic action — Peter's ears flat, legs pumping, blue jacket flying.

pages.english_text: Peter's jacket got caught in a net. He was stuck!
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_014.jpg] Peter tangled and struggling in a gooseberry net, his blue jacket caught in the mesh. He pulls and twists but cannot free himself. A desperate, frightened moment.

pages.english_text: Sparrows flew to him. "Keep trying!" they chirped.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_015.jpg] Peter bursting free from the net as small sparrows flutter around him encouragingly. His jacket tears as he pulls loose. Birds circle as if cheering him on.

pages.english_text: Peter pulled free, but he lost his jacket and shoes.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_016.jpg] McGregor's boot and a sieve slamming down just where Peter was — Peter barely escaping. Birds scatter. The jacket left behind. A near-miss moment of danger.

pages.english_text: Peter hid in a watering can full of cold water!
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_017.jpg] Peter hiding inside a large green watering can, only his ears and front paws sticking out. He is drenched and shivering. A funny, desperate hiding spot.

pages.english_text: Mr. McGregor looked everywhere for Peter.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_018.jpg] Mr. McGregor searching inside a garden shed, looking behind pots and containers. The watering can where Peter hides is visible. Tense — will he find Peter?

pages.english_text: Peter sneaked out and knocked over three flowerpots. Crash!
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_019.jpg] Peter crashing into flowerpots, knocking them over with a clatter. McGregor's boot visible at edge — he heard the noise! Terracotta pots tumbling and breaking.

pages.english_text: Peter was lost. He could not find the gate.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_020.jpg] Peter standing upright by a garden wall, looking around anxiously. Alone, alert, ears up, trying to figure out which way to go. Lost and frightened.

pages.english_text: He asked a little mouse for help, but she could not help him.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_021.jpg] Peter leaning exhausted against a blue locked door in the garden wall. A tiny mouse sits at his feet. Peter looks down hopefully, but the door is locked.

pages.english_text: Peter saw a white cat. He stayed away from her.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_022.jpg] A white cat sitting still by a garden pond. Peter visible peeking cautiously from behind a watering can. He wisely avoids the cat — a moment of prudence at last.

pages.english_text: Then Peter saw the gate at last!
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_023.jpg] Peter crouching behind a wheelbarrow in a cabbage patch. Mr. McGregor in background hoeing with his back turned. The garden gate visible beyond. Peter sees his chance!

pages.english_text: He ran to the gate and squeezed under it. Free!
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_024.jpg] Peter sprinting and squeezing under the garden gate to escape. His body halfway through. Freedom on the other side. Mirrors page 6 — the escape.

pages.english_text: Peter's jacket became a scarecrow in the garden.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_025.jpg] Peter's little blue jacket and shoes on a scarecrow in the garden. Birds perch on the scarecrow's arms. A quiet, humorous aftermath — Peter lost his clothes.

pages.english_text: Peter ran all the way home. He was very tired.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_026.jpg] Mrs. Rabbit in the kitchen with a pot. Peter collapsed nearby, exhausted, lying flat. Home but utterly spent. No jacket, no shoes, just a sick little rabbit.

pages.english_text: Mother put Peter to bed with chamomile tea.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_001.jpg] Mrs. Rabbit giving Peter medicine or tea in bed. Peter lies looking miserable. Mother tends to him lovingly despite his disobedience — consequences, but still loved.

pages.english_text: But the good rabbits had bread and milk for supper.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_028.jpg] The three good sister rabbits sitting happily at a table, eating bread and milk. They obeyed. They get the reward. Clear contrast with poor Peter in bed.
